jokes.text: I'm going home, by myself, as is normal for me. Have a good night, Leela! I won't see you again until the morning.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to act casual about leaving work
jokes.punchline: Overexplaining his normal routine in a suspicious way that clearly indicates he's up to something
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat - Fry's awkward overcompensation when trying to hide something is very in-character

jokes.text: Professor, you live here. Where are you going? I have no idea!
jokes.setup: Professor is leaving work but lives at work
jokes.punchline: His cheerful admission that he has no clue where he's going
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth absent-mindedness delivered with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: Ah, alone and unhooked. Let the evening begin.
jokes.setup: Bender finally being alone
jokes.punchline: His relief at being 'unhooked' (disconnected from others) like it's some kind of bondage
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for Bender's antisocial nature, using robot terminology for human emotions

jokes.text: Top Ramen in a Cremora sauce... Xerox-blackened chicken... cherry throat lozenge jubilee
jokes.setup: Fry describing his romantic dinner made from office supplies
jokes.punchline: Each dish gets progressively more absurd, ending with throat lozenges as dessert
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of increasingly disgusting office-supply food items with great specificity

jokes.text: What a wonderful nighttime sneezing coughing stuffy head so-I-can-rest dessert!
jokes.setup: Fry's throat lozenge dessert
jokes.punchline: Leela reciting the Nyquil slogan as if it's a romantic description
jokes.explanation: Clever use of familiar advertising slogan in unexpected romantic context

jokes.text: If you can't see me, is that private?
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela asking for privacy
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's literal interpretation of privacy while still being visible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy logic - technical compliance while missing the point entirely

jokes.text: On Batman's night off. Oh! This is a stickup! Oh, hey, Fry and Leela.
jokes.setup: Fry commenting on the dangerous area being safe on Batman's night off
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting mugged by their friend Bender who casually greets them
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup-payoff with perfect character integration - of course Bender would mug his friends

jokes.text: Which reminds me, this is still a mugging, so... Hee-ya! Ow!
jokes.setup: Bender being friendly during the mugging
jokes.punchline: Remembering he's supposed to be robbing them and half-heartedly continuing
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the mugging bit with Bender's casual criminality

jokes.text: From work, and LinkedIn?
jokes.setup: Zoidberg identifying himself to his coworkers
jokes.punchline: Adding LinkedIn as if it's equivalent to working together
jokes.explanation: Modern social media reference in futuristic context creates mild anachronistic humor

jokes.text: ♪ Dishes, dishes, dishes ♪ ♪ Cleaning up the dishes. ♪ I'm a singing busboy. No, you're not!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg trying to be a singing busboy
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt denial of his self-proclaimed status
jokes.explanation: Typical Zoidberg delusion meets typical Leela directness

jokes.text: ♪ Just the two of us ♪ ♪ We can make it if we try ♪ ♪ Just the two of us... ♪ Uh, what about...?
jokes.setup: Fry playing romantic music about 'just the two of us'
jokes.punchline: Leela noticing they're clearly not alone (presumably the Professor is visible)
jokes.explanation: Simple ironic contradiction between the song lyrics and reality

jokes.text: He won't wake up if we stay quiet. Ooh... Ah! Nibbler! Scat!
jokes.setup: Fry assuring they can be intimate quietly
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting loud when Nibbler interferes
jokes.explanation: Standard setup-payoff with immediate contradiction of the plan

jokes.text: Only if I cut off my arm. I'll get a knife.
jokes.setup: Fry saying he can only turn off ads by cutting off his arm
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately volunteering to get a knife
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's pragmatic approach to extreme solutions

jokes.text: As a return guest, Leela will receive a 25% discount and a complimentary waterfall.
jokes.setup: Standard vacation package advertising
jokes.punchline: Offering a complimentary waterfall as if it's a hotel amenity
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of resort amenities to impossible natural features

jokes.text: Oh, not the whole time.
jokes.setup: Fry worried Leela would think of Sean the whole time
jokes.punchline: Leela's honest admission she'd only think of him part of the time
jokes.explanation: Perfect uncomfortable honesty that makes the situation worse, very in-character

jokes.text: The inhabitants of Simian 7-- the Planet of the Apes. And monkeys. And tarsiers, and several lemurs. All primates, really.
jokes.setup: Professor giving the dramatic name 'Planet of the Apes'
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflating it by listing all the other less impressive primates
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of sci-fi drama with mundane specificity, very Professor-like

jokes.text: We're not cattle, we're human beings. You're human beings? Good Lord, you can't go on this delivery.
jokes.setup: Leela asserting their humanity to demand rights
jokes.punchline: Professor's horror at learning they're human, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - asserting humanity backfires completely

jokes.text: Hooray! I'm the Fry, with the cool red jacket and the walking on sunshine!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg getting to replace Fry on the mission
jokes.punchline: His confused enthusiasm and weird description of Fry's attributes
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg misunderstanding of human expressions and excitement

jokes.text: Amy, what are you? Human. Damn!
jokes.setup: Professor asking Amy's species hopefully
jokes.punchline: His disappointment at the obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to obvious information being unwelcome

jokes.text: Do you have a marmoset costume? No. Just these marmoset pajamas. They'll have to do.
jokes.setup: Need for a marmoset costume
jokes.punchline: The completely inadequate substitute being accepted as sufficient
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic of pajamas being an acceptable substitute for a disguise

jokes.text: Man, these hands do everything but pick your nose. Wow. Well picked.
jokes.setup: Fry saying the robot hands can't pick his nose
jokes.punchline: Immediate evidence that they can and did
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-payoff with immediate contradiction and gross-out element

jokes.text: Somebody might see Wingus and the Ping Pong boys!
jokes.setup: Fry being modest about skinny-dipping
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous euphemism for his genitals
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly creative and character-appropriate euphemism that's both childish and oddly sophisticated

jokes.text: Marco! Polo! Marco! Polo! Sean? Leela? Sean?! Polo!
jokes.setup: Innocent Marco Polo game between Fry and Leela
jokes.punchline: Sean's unexpected arrival, with him still saying 'Polo' despite the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from innocent game to awkward interruption, with Sean's continued 'Polo' adding to the humor

jokes.text: Repeat customer discount, free meals 'cause I brought my own peanut butter. Whole trip cost less than 600 bucks.
jokes.setup: Sean explaining his cheap vacation
jokes.punchline: Increasingly pathetic details about his frugality
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Sean's extreme cheapness through specific details

jokes.text: But, hey, they gave me two free drink coupons for my trouble. You want to buy one?
jokes.setup: Sean got compensation for his inconvenience
jokes.punchline: Trying to sell his free drink coupon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sean characterization - so cheap he tries to monetize free compensation

jokes.text: Nude and interrupted.
jokes.setup: Leela asking what their relationship status is
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt summary of their current awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and deadpan delivery that perfectly captures the absurd situation

jokes.text: Put a little peanut butter on there, they really fill you up.
jokes.setup: Sean talking about unlimited pretzels
jokes.punchline: His pathetic life hack to make free bar snacks into a meal
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Sean's pathetic cheapness with specific detail about surviving on bar snacks

jokes.text: Sure, that'd be great. I'll just sit here and feed the mosquitoes.
jokes.setup: Fry agreeing to let Leela hang out with her ex
jokes.punchline: His passive-aggressive complaint disguised as agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's tendency toward martyrdom and poor communication

jokes.text: We don't want the cars, just the tires! Gentlemen, the new office chairs are here.
jokes.setup: Question about why monkeys want classic cars
jokes.punchline: They're using the tires as office furniture
jokes.explanation: Creative visual gag that subverts expectations about what monkeys would want from classic cars

jokes.text: You know, normally I don't go for New World monkeys, but I am digging your upright posture.
jokes.setup: Monkey hitting on Amy in marmoset disguise
jokes.punchline: Using taxonomic classifications as pickup line criteria
jokes.explanation: Clever use of scientific classification in a pickup line context

jokes.text: Why don't you just show her your big blue ass?
jokes.setup: Edwards hitting on Amy inappropriately
jokes.punchline: Coworker's crude suggestion referencing actual primate behavior
jokes.explanation: Appropriately crude workplace comment that references real animal behavior

jokes.text: Working in financial services, you really get to help rich people realize their expensive dreams.
jokes.setup: Guenter describing his job satisfaction
jokes.punchline: The completely self-aware cynical description of financial services
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on financial services industry delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: I recorded a demo and shot a video, but then I lost my phone.
jokes.setup: Sean talking about his music career taking off
jokes.punchline: The anticlimactic derailment over losing his phone
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of apparent success story with mundane failure

jokes.text: Say, I don't suppose you're free later?
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about being stuck with mechanical hands
jokes.punchline: The hands propositioning him
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment giving personality to inanimate objects

jokes.text: Fry, there's nothing to be jealous of. I've been sitting here paying my bills online.
jokes.setup: Fry jealous about Sean and Leela's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Sean's completely unromantic activity during their 'date'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sean characterization - so practical he does chores during romantic moments

jokes.text: Dude, you broke my reed! You owe me, like, a dollar! You'll have to kill me for it!
jokes.setup: Fry breaking Sean's saxophone reed
jokes.punchline: Sean's disproportionate concern over a dollar vs Fry's disproportionate response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' ridiculous priorities

jokes.text: I've had that reed for 20 years! It's irreplaceable!
jokes.setup: Sean upset about a dollar reed
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's kept a consumable item for decades out of cheapness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail showing Sean's extreme frugality extending to hoarding disposable items

jokes.text: This is my wife, Darlene. We met at the 99-cent store.
jokes.setup: Sean introducing his wife
jokes.punchline: The appropriately cheap location where they met
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - of course Sean would meet his wife at a discount store

jokes.text: One eye, purple hair-- you're Leela! The crazy one that Sean dumped.
jokes.setup: Darlene recognizing Leela
jokes.punchline: Her tactless identification and immediate insult
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward confrontation humor, effective but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Listen, honey, I'd love to stay here and Denny's-fight you, but they just told me the shuttle's fixed.
jokes.setup: Darlene about to fight Leela
jokes.punchline: Using 'Denny's-fight' as a verb for low-class confrontation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coinage - 'Denny's-fight' perfectly captures a specific type of trashy confrontation

jokes.text: I'm drunk, I'm grouchy, and I'm gonna eat this peanut butter. Want to watch? Not really, but I will.
jokes.setup: Sean announcing his pathetic evening plans
jokes.punchline: Fry's honest but resigned agreement to watch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Sean's sad priorities and Fry's morbid curiosity

jokes.text: Humans experimented on us, chained us to organ-grinders, made us ride around in ridiculous tiny vehicles. You're still riding around in ridiculous tiny vehicles. By choice.
jokes.setup: Monkey complaining about human treatment including tiny vehicles
jokes.punchline: Amy pointing out they still use tiny vehicles, monkey's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that highlights the absurdity of the monkeys' grievances

jokes.text: Ew, gross! I'm gonna use the tongs.
jokes.setup: All-you-can-eat buffet where you pick food off yourself
jokes.punchline: Amy's civilized response to the disgusting buffet concept
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor with Amy as the reasonable straight-man

jokes.text: The animals don't even know they're in captivity. See?
jokes.setup: Guide explaining their humane zoo practices
jokes.punchline: Revealing Fry and Leela are the zoo animals who don't know they're captive
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode with perfect ironic setup

jokes.text: Oh, your God! Fry and Leela are in a zoo! Shh! Oh, sorry.
jokes.setup: Bender's loud realization about the zoo situation
jokes.punchline: Amy shushing him, Bender immediately apologizing quietly
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Bender's uncharacteristic consideration for the situation

jokes.text: I've always wanted to make love by bug-light. Shmoly crap! We've got to get Fry and Leela out of this habitat before they humiliate themselves.
jokes.setup: Leela's romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's horrified realization about what they're about to witness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup with Zoidberg as the unlikely voice of propriety

jokes.text: Mr. Zoo Director, we don't know you and you're facing away from us, but you have our friends caged up and... Dr. Banjo?!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg addressing the unknown zoo director
jokes.punchline: Recognizing him as someone they know when he turns around
jokes.explanation: Standard recognition reveal with good setup acknowledging the dramatic convention

jokes.text: I've never seen one have so much trouble with zippers.
jokes.setup: Dr. Banjo discussing the human exhibit's popularity
jokes.punchline: Highlighting Fry's specific incompetence as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific detail about Fry's incompetence being amusing to zoo visitors

jokes.text: Zoidberg and his awful stench. Stanky. Bender and his obnoxious ego. But-but... And what about Amy? Always prancing around in those stupid marmoset pajamas.
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela thinking they're alone and can speak freely
jokes.punchline: Their harsh but accurate assessments of their friends who are listening
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with accurate character assessments that hurt because they're true

jokes.text: You know who I hate most? That monkey we haven't seen in years. Guenter.
jokes.setup: Fry listing people he dislikes
jokes.punchline: Singling out someone they rarely see, while Guenter is right there
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with Fry insulting someone who's present but disguised

jokes.text: Wild human! Wild human!
jokes.setup: Amy being discovered as human in disguise
jokes.punchline: The panic over a 'wild human' loose in the zoo
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal with humans being the dangerous animals that cause panic

jokes.text: Even if my stupid marmoset pajamas offend Leela's big fat eye.
jokes.setup: Amy deciding to help despite being insulted
jokes.punchline: Her petty retaliation while still doing the right thing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's hurt feelings but ultimate loyalty

jokes.text: After they said I had an obnoxious ego? Me, the magnificent Bender?
jokes.setup: Bender upset about being called egotistical
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating his ego while denying it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - the complete lack of self-awareness while proving the point exactly

jokes.text: Back! Back, you beasts! Follow me. The one who stinks, according to Fry's delicate nose.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg leading the rescue despite being insulted
jokes.punchline: His bitter reference to the insult while still helping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Zoidberg's hurt but continued loyalty

jokes.text: Prairie elephant! Adorable prairie elephant!
jokes.setup: Amy trying to distract from the chase
jokes.punchline: Her obvious attempt to redirect attention with fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Standard distraction attempt humor, not particularly creative

jokes.text: And the robot hands would make you a strawberry shortcake any time of day. 4:00, 7:00, any time. No way! I'm not lying.
jokes.setup: Fry describing the robot hands' capabilities
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific time examples and defensive insistence
jokes.explanation: Mild callback with Fry's characteristic over-explanation of simple concepts

jokes.text: Listen to Sparkles.
jokes.setup: Fry agreeing with Amy's advice
jokes.punchline: Still calling her by her fake marmoset name
jokes.explanation: Small callback moment showing Fry's continued confusion about the disguise

jokes.text: Also, nothing in here smells like a rotting crab.
jokes.setup: Fry commenting positively on their current situation
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment referencing Zoidberg's absence
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier insults, showing Fry hasn't learned to be more considerate

jokes.text: You were in a zoo!
jokes.setup: The entire episode's buildup to the reveal
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's excited delivery of the truth they were oblivious to
jokes.explanation: Strong episode-ending reveal that recontextualizes everything with perfect delivery timing
